verdict_reason: Cross-tag coherence: 2.42's tags (vanity-metrics, feedback-loops, metrics-discipline) are coherent with 2.48's (operator-system-coupling, incident-response, team-state) — both verses warn against the operator's reading of metric/system-state being captured by attachment. 2.42 names the partial-reading pathology; 2.48 names the operator-state coupling that produces it. Both belong to the same doctrinal arc. The cross-references in the source pack (to 2.43 syntactic continuation, to 2.46 thematic arc — small water-pond scoping the same Veda-utility question) all resolve and support the citing claim: 2.46's 'the realised brahmana's relation to the Vedas' closes the same scoping arc that 2.42-45 opens. The engineering layer preserves this arc visibility. PASS for both 6.1 and 6.5.
